kotonia.enginePath.markdownDescription: Path to the `kotonia-cli` engine binary. Resolved on the extension host (i.e. the remote/WSL host when connected). Leave as `kotonia-cli` to use PATH, or point at a debug build like `${workspaceFolder}/target/debug/kotonia-cli`.

kotonia.model.markdownDescription: Model id passed to the engine. Hosted (GPU-free, needs `KOTONIA_API_KEY`): `kotonia-gemma4-26b`, `kotonia-v4-flash`. Local: `gemma4-26b-uncensored`, `deepseek-v4-flash`. DeepSeek API: `deepseek-chat`, `deepseek-reasoner`.

kotonia.approvalMode.markdownDescription: `all` gates every command, `allowlist` auto-runs read-only/build/test families and gates destructive ones, `auto` runs everything without asking.

kotonia.workspaceMode.markdownDescription: `worktree` (default) runs the agent in an isolated git worktree so your working copy is untouched until you merge. `in-place` edits the workspace directly (edits show live in the editor, but no isolation).

kotonia.extraArgs.description: Extra CLI arguments appended to the engine spawn (e.g. ["--max-iterations", "50"]).

kotonia.language.markdownDescription: Default reply language for the agent (BCP-47-ish code, e.g. `ja`, `en`, `zh`). Injected as a one-line instruction on the first turn of each session; the agent still switches if you write in another language. Empty = no language steering.

kotonia.avatar.enabled.markdownDescription: Show a talking lip-sync avatar (Ditto) that speaks each final answer. Streams from kotonia.ai `/api/voice/ditto/tts/stream/avatar` using your device login. Requires a registered `kotonia.avatar.id` and the Ditto/TTS servers running.

kotonia.avatar.id.markdownDescription: Registered Ditto avatar id. Defaults to the Kotona mascot (`kotona_v4`). Use **Kotonia: アバターを選択 / Select Avatar** to pick from the avatars registered on the server (ことな / ひなた / …) or enter any id. `kotona_v1`–`v3`, `hinata_*`, and your own (via `/api/voice/ditto/prepare`) also work.

kotonia.avatar.ttsBackend.description: TTS backend the avatar voice uses. The Kotona mascot uses AivisSpeech.

kotonia.avatar.speaker.description: Speaker/voice id passed to the TTS backend (backend-specific). Numeric ids (e.g. AivisSpeech まお-あまあま = 888753762) are sent as numbers. Empty = backend default.

kotonia.avatar.speed.description: Speech speed for the avatar voice (1.0 = normal).

kotonia.avatar.language.description: Language hint for the TTS backend (e.g. ja, en, zh).

kotonia.avatar.apiBase.markdownDescription: Override the API base for avatar streaming. Empty = use the server from your device login (`~/.kotonia/daemon.json`), falling back to `https://kotonia.ai`.
